sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
கலையாத கல்வி தந்திடும் தாயே
கலைவாணியே கமலப் பதமலர் தொழுதேன்
sections.translit:
kalaiyAdha kalvi thandhidum thAyE
kalaivANiyE kamalap padhamalar thozhudhEn
sections.en: Oh, Mother and Goddess of knowledge, who bestows indestructible knowledge, I worshiped your lotus feet.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
நிலை உயர நிகரிலா கலை அருள்வாய்
இலை ஈடென அடியரை நிலை நாட்டிடுவாய்
sections.translit:
nilai uyara nigarilA kalai aruLvAy
ilai Idena adiyarai nilai nAttiduvAy
sections.en: Please offer incomparable skills in the arts to upgrade my stature; establish your devotees as unique.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
மாமுனி சினம் தாங்கி அன்பனைக் காத்து
தாரணிதனில் சரஸ்வதியாய் ஓடிய ஸ்படிகமே
அக மதில் மாய்த்து கரமதில் ஏந்திய ஞானமீந்து
அகமதில் நிலைத்து அன்னையே உன்னையே பாடிட
sections.translit:
mAmuni sinam thAngi anbanaik kAtthu
dhAraNithanil saraswathiyAy Odiya spadigamE
aga madhil mAytthu karamadhil Endhiya nyAnamIndhu
agamadhil nilaitthu annaiyE unnaiyE pAdida
sections.en: You protected the devotee Vasishta from the anger of the great sage, Viswamitra, and you ran in this world as the pure and clear Saraswathi river. I sing your glory and worship your lotus feet in order for you, Mother, to destroy the artificial fortress of ego and offer me the knowledge that you carry in your hand, so you can dwell in my heart forever. Author’s Note Reference is made in the charanam of this song to frequent quarrels between the sages Vasishta and Vishwamitra. Once, the angry Vishwamitra ordered Goddess Saraswathi to bring Vasishta to him on the other side of the river bank with an evil intention to kill him. She carried Vasishta to him but still gets cursed by the angry sage. Due to this curse, she ran as the bloody and tainted river Aruna for a while. Contemptuous of the deaths in the Kurukshetra war between relatives, teachers, and students, and the trends due to the inhabitation of rakshasas on the river bank and disillusioned by the changing cultures, it is believed that Saraswati disappeared from earth around Mahabharata time. This episode of the conflict between the two rishis is vividly laid out in Balarama Theertha Yatra Parva of Mahabharata. The poet has also used this story in a Navarasa piece on Goddess Saraswathi. For details of the Vasishta Viswamitra conflict and the tribulations of river Saraswathi, see the links: Saraswati River Legend River Saraswati turns bloody Aruna
